title: Indira Nehru Gandhi v. Raj Narain
legal_principles: Free and fair elections as basic structure
summary: After Allahabad High Court found PM Indira Gandhi guilty of corrupt practices in elections, the 39th Amendment retroactively validated her election and placed disputes regarding PM's election beyond judicial review. Supreme Court struck down the amendment as violating free and fair elections — part of the basic structure.
key_holdings: Free and fair elections are part of the basic structure of the Constitution
Parliament cannot validate its own elections retrospectively
Rule of law and separation of powers are basic structure elements
Clause 4 of 39th Amendment struck down as unconstitutional
key_quotes: Democracy postulates that free and fair elections must be held.
Rule of law is the basic feature of our Constitution.
why_important: Applied and extended basic structure doctrine to election law. Led directly to declaration of Emergency in 1975.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Establishes that legislative overreach cannot validate improper financial or administrative decisions retrospectively.
